title: Stack Navigator
description: Navigate between screens with push/pop transitions.

slides.title: Stack Navigator Basics 📚
slides.content:
# What is a Stack?

A stack navigator manages a stack of screens. When you navigate to a new screen, it's pushed on top. Pressing the back button pops it off.

Think of a deck of cards – you can only see the top card.

## Creating a Stack

First, import the necessary functions:

```jsx
import { createStackNavigator } from '@react-navigation/stack';
const Stack = createStackNavigator();
```

slides.title: The `navigation` Prop
slides.content:
## Navigating

Every screen component receives a `navigation` prop (if it's registered as a screen). It has methods like:

* `navigation.navigate('RouteName')` – go to a screen, pushing if not already in stack, or going back if it exists.
* `navigation.push('RouteName')` – always push a new instance.
* `navigation.goBack()` – pop the current screen.
* `navigation.popToTop()` – go back to the first screen in the stack.

Try them out!

quizData.question: What does `navigation.navigate('Details')` do if 'Details' is already in the stack?

options.text: It pushes a new Details screen
It goes back to the existing Details screen

quizData.explanation: `navigate` tries to go to an existing instance; `push` always adds a new one.

slides.title: Header Configuration 🎨
Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Wrap your app in `<NavigationContainer>` only once, at the root. It manages the navigation state and deep links.
